- [TAP5-1043](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1043) – PropertyDisplayBlocks causes NullPointerException when property of type Calendar is null
- [TAP5-1047](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1047) – @IncludeJavaScriptLibrary and @IncludeStylesheet may incorrectly calculate resources in component sub-classes
- [TAP5-1052](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1052) – Component classes page of the guide should be much more specific that only component classes go in pages, components, etc.
- [TAP5-1054](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1054) – Not able to pass an empty string as appName to PageTester
- [TAP5-1057](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1057) – XSS vulnerability in calendar component
- [TAP5-1060](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1060) – In IE8, forms that should submit using Ajax are triggering full page requests due to a client-side JavaScript error
- [TAP5-1067](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1067) – Created component constructor may use too many parameters
- [TAP5-1068](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1068) – RenderSupport.addScriptLink(String) deprecated and removed without replacement
- [TAP5-1069](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1069) – Tapestry POM should reference only the Apache Nexus repository, not the tapestry.formos.com repository
- [TAP5-1074](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1074) – The @QueryParameter annotation uses TypeCoercer to convert from request parameter strings to method parameter values, it should use the ValueEncoder
- [TAP5-1075](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1075) – When a template contains a reference to an unknown component, the new UnknownValueException is reported but the Location is no longer reported, making it much harder to determine where the error occurred
- [TAP5-1076](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1076) – When a service implementation is reloadable, it will not eager load
- [TAP5-1080](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1080) – Page activation context lost when redirecting from HTTP to HTTPS due to the @Secure annotation
- [TAP5-1086](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1086) – Localized root path request results in a 404 error if Index page is not available
- [TAP5-1088](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1088) – Bean editor model for <class name> already contains a property model for property <property name> when combining model parameter with add parameter of BeanEditor or BeanEditForm
- [TAP5-1090](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1090) – Deleting a live-reloaded class can result in a non-descriptive "Parameter url was null." exception
- [TAP5-1096](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1096) – If a Zone's id parameter is bound, then the clientId property should return that value, even before the Zone renders
- [TAP5-1100](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1100) – On ajax request, template body doesn't render if page is new in pagepool or if server restarted
- [TAP5-1105](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1105) – BeanModelSource should recognize public field as properties, but doesn't
- [TAP5-1106](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1106) – Tapestry.ajaxFailureHandler has an incorrect signature for an Ajax.Request onException callback
- [TAP5-1109](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1109) – Updating multiple zones within a Form creates anomalous empty text fields
- [TAP5-1110](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1110) – Tapestry holds onto the verbose descriptions of component class transformations, causing a memory leak
- [TAP5-1116](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1116) – Live lock when using compressed and virtual assets due to ByteArrayOutputStream being synchronized
- [TAP5-1120](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1120) – It is not possible to override the default Translator contributions to the TranslatorSource service
- [TAP5-1124](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1124) – FormEventManager.setSubmittingElement(element) creates input element with type="input"
- [TAP5-1128](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1128) – A space is added to a HTML start comment sequence making it impossible to add IE conditional comments
- [TAP5-1130](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1130) – LinkSubmit component doesn't work when form contains Select component with default id
- [TAP5-1136](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1136) – java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException from UpdateListenerHub when a weak reference is reclaimed
- [TAP5-1137](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1137) – Dynamically adding JavaScript libraries to a page via a partial page update does not seem to work consistently in Safari and Chrome
- [TAP5-1138](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1138) – ClassTransformation deprecated a bunch of methods, then refers to replacements that don't exist
- [TAP5-1141](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1141) – Type coercion from arbitrary object to Boolean will NPE when the object's toString() returns null (should coerce to false)
- [TAP5-1145](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1145) – Writing only text (or raw text) into a Document via MarkupWriter results in [empty Document], not the actual text
- [TAP5-1147](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1147) – Ajax updates under IE leak lots of memory
- [TAP5-1154](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1154) – ValidationDecorator.insideField not called for Select component
- [TAP5-1156](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1156) – Provide a ValueEncoder for a Hibernate entity automatically only if a mapped class exists
- [TAP5-1158](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1158) – Captured HTML from failed Selenium assertions do not get stored in the correct place on Windows
- [TAP5-1161](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1161) – Tapestry 5.1 seems incompatible with Spring 3
- [TAP5-1166](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1166) – ClassPropertyAdapterImpl should skip static fields
- [TAP5-1182](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1182) – javax.management preventsTapestry apps from being deployed on GAE
- [TAP5-1185](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1185) – Upgrade to latest Javassist version to avoid LocalVariableTable exceptions (especially on Google App Engine)
- [TAP5-1198](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1198) – A user defined activate event handler appears to execute \*before\* the event handler supplied by @PageActivationContext, making defensive coding impossible
- [TAP5-1203](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1203) – Use of @Contribute annotation does not work properly with marker annotations
- [TAP5-1207](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1207) – A form control component (such as TextField) whose id is "id" can confuse client-side logic for the Form DOM object
- [TAP5-1209](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1209) – Marker annotations used with services that have contributions should be applicable to methods (as well as fields and parameters) for compatibility with @Contribute
- [TAP5-1210](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1210) – MultiZone update from EventLink with Form fails in Internet Explorer
- [TAP5-1211](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1211) – LinkSubmit broken in IE 7 mode
- [TAP5-1216](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1216) – ValueEncoderSource does not define a @UsesMappedConfiguration annotation
- [TAP5-1223](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1223) – The name "JavaScript" should have a capital "S" but is inconsistent in some class and interface names
- [TAP5-1224](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1224) – When contributing to a service using a "contribute" method, the service id from the method name now matches case-sensitively to the service id (it should be case insensitive)
- [TAP5-1262](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1262) – XSS vulnerability in calendar component (apply to 5.1.0.x)
- [TAP5-1282](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1282) – Form component generates invalid xHtml: there should not be the "name" attribute - Applied to 5.1

<a id="release-notes-520--releasenotes5.2.0-improvementsmade"></a>
<a id="release-notes-520--improvements-made"></a>

### Improvements Made

- [TAP5-69](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-69) – Add annotation, @Contribute, to allow service contributor methods to be arbitrary named
- [TAP5-88](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-88) – Add support for Bulgarian locale
- [TAP5-179](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-179) – The TriggerFragment mixin would be more useful if it could invert its logic, i.e., make a a fragment visible when a checkbox was turned off
- [TAP5-247](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-247) – Move all Tapestry 5 configuration keys to a constant class
- [TAP5-335](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-335) – Provide access to annotations of service implementation class
- [TAP5-424](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-424) – Allow component libraries to contribute extra resources to the global application catalog
- [TAP5-632](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-632) – Property names (in property expressions) should be able to read or update public variables
- [TAP5-674](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-674) – Make it easy to implement a page callback mechanism
- [TAP5-678](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-678) – Allow blackbird to be disabled in production mode
- [TAP5-685](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-685) – Allow services defined by module tapestry-ioc to be advised and decorated
- [TAP5-713](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-713) – Change template parser to not use StAX, as it is not (yet) compatible with Google App Engine
- [TAP5-760](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-760) – The Form event "validateForm" is awkwardly named and should be replaced with the simpler name "validate"
- [TAP5-762](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-762) – Upgrade Selenium dependencies to version 1.0.1
- [TAP5-764](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-764) – Hidden should support ClientElement and support informal parameters.
- [TAP5-777](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-777) – Tapestry should ensure that mixins are applied in a deterministic order.
- [TAP5-783](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-783) – tapestry should mark via annotation which annotations are component-specific vs. more general
- [TAP5-789](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-789) – Provide bean and display blocks for java.util.Calendar
- [TAP5-790](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-790) – Provide ApplicationStatePersistenceStrategy for Hibernate entities
- [TAP5-803](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-803) – ProgressiveDisplay should include a read-only body property of type Block, just like Zone
- [TAP5-807](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-807) – PageRenderLinkSource should add additional methods for creating a Link when you have the page's activation context as an EventContext
- [TAP5-810](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-810) – Improve documentation of @Persist annotation
- [TAP5-811](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-811) – Layout component documentation is not very clear about directory structure
- [TAP5-814](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-814) – include a diagram showing how incoming requests pass through the different pipelines, filters and dispatchers
- [TAP5-826](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-826) – The ActionLink documentation is missing the option to use object arrays as context inside templates
- [TAP5-859](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-859) – In a stack trace, any lines associated with line #1 of a class are likely to be synthetic methods and should be classified as "uninteresting"
- [TAP5-863](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-863) – Tapestry.ajaxRequest is insufficient when doing extra, such as adding query parameters
- [TAP5-884](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-884) – Introduce a symbol for tapestry.js
- [TAP5-887](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-887) – Easier way to initialize a JSONObject with keys and values
- [TAP5-889](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-889) – Provide fluent API for order constraints of contributions
- [TAP5-904](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-904) – Make use of Selenium.showContextualBanner() when running integration tests
- [TAP5-905](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-905) – Tapestry should support the full range of Unicode characters acceptible by Java as property names
- [TAP5-912](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-912) – Validation of properties of type java.util.Collection should fail when the collection is empty
- [TAP5-914](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-914) – When autobuilding a Java bean (such as a Session State Object), the code should use the OperationTracker
- [TAP5-915](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-915) – It should be possible to override a components message catalog
- [TAP5-917](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-917) – Don't set Expires header when tapestry.production-mode is false
- [TAP5-927](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-927) – Cannot use Scala for Tapestry IOC Modules
- [TAP5-948](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-948) – Built-in mechanism to identify self-referential links and/or event/render requests
- [TAP5-958](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-958) – Upgrade Tapestry's built-in copy of prototype.js to version 1.6.1
- [TAP5-963](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-963) – Allow access to static resources (css, js, jpg, jpeg, png, gif) inside the app package
- [TAP5-965](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-965) – Upgrade EasyMock dependency to release 2.5.2
- [TAP5-967](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-967) – ObjectLocator.autobuild would be more useful with an override that allowed a message about the object to be described
- [TAP5-971](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-971) – FormFragment component should include a parameter to control whether non-visible content is included in the form submission
- [TAP5-978](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-978) – Provide remote management of the page pool settings
- [TAP5-979](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-979) – Form component should be more careful with the validation tracker to ensure that a session is not created unless needed
- [TAP5-993](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-993) – Reorganize ComponentClassTransformWorkers to start moving away from Javassist
- [TAP5-994](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-994) – Field autofocus should be done via Tapestry.init instead of using $ function
- [TAP5-1000](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1000) – When Autobuilding a class, the constructor to be used should be identified in the trace output
- [TAP5-1014](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1014) – Message about missing or wrong retention policy of a marker annotation is misleading
- [TAP5-1015](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1015) – Provide a new return type for event handler methods that would trigger the rendering of a particular page without a redirect
- [TAP5-1023](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1023) – (5.2.0-SPNAPSHOT Maven dependencies) tapestry-hibernate-5.2.0 jar generated with tapestry-hibernate-5.2.0-yyyyMMdd.hhmmss-?.jar > tapestry-hibernate-5.2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar whereas all other jar are using -5.2.0-SNAPSHOT
- [TAP5-1024](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1024) – Submit component should allow return values from event handler methods triggered by its event
- [TAP5-1033](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1033) – Upgrade selenium dependencies to 1.0.3
- [TAP5-1036](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1036) – It would be nice if there was a way for committers to run individual integration tests again
- [TAP5-1037](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1037) – Rewrite URLRewriter integration tests to use new SeleniumTestCase instead of deprecated AbstractIntegrationTestSuite
- [TAP5-1038](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1038) – Rewrite live reload integration tests to use new SeleniumTestCase instead of deprecated AbstractIntegrationTestSuite
- [TAP5-1046](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1046) – Change Tapestry client-side JavaScript to make the tapx/Confirm component easier to implement
- [TAP5-1050](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1050) – Add API to prevent a particular service implementation from being live reloaded
- [TAP5-1055](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1055) – Provide hook to post-process properties files before rolling them into component Messages
- [TAP5-1061](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1061) – When a Zone component sends an Ajax request for a client-side update, it should pass an extra query parameter identifying the zone's client-side id
- [TAP5-1077](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1077) – Merge symbol values into property values from a bean definition
- [TAP5-1085](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1085) – The ExceptionReporter should be configured to mark more of the Tapestry generated classes (used for method & field access and method advice) as hidden by default
- [TAP5-1091](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1091) – CLONE -Handler method of LinkSubmit component should accept a context
- [TAP5-1094](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1094) – Create a binding prefix, "symbol:", that is used to access IoC symbols
- [TAP5-1112](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1112) – Handle array types in property expressions
- [TAP5-1126](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1126) – Add a new validator, "none", used when overriding the @Validate annotation
- [TAP5-1127](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1127) – Documentation for the Submit and LinkSubmit components should identify that you should cancel defer when inside a Loop
- [TAP5-1129](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1129) – LinkSubmit should render minimally (and as <span> tag) and then fill in the details on the client
- [TAP5-1132](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1132) – HibernateGridDataSource should assign the result of Projections.rowCount() to java.lang.Number when determining the number of rows for the configured entity type
- [TAP5-1135](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1135) – Provide a convinient method to build and start the registry
- [TAP5-1150](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1150) – Refactor out a new base class between org.testng.Assert and org.apache.tapestry5.ioc.test.TestBase for non-mock related tests
- [TAP5-1152](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1152) – TypeCoercer should include a method exposing the coercion it would use for a given source and target type
- [TAP5-1153](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1153) – When in development mode, Tapestry should pretty-print JSON content
- [TAP5-1155](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1155) – JavaScript initialization inside the partial page render Ajax response should be unquoted
- [TAP5-1157](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1157) – Client-side exceptions during Tapestry.init() should be caught and reported with the console
- [TAP5-1165](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1165) – Make better use of the OperationTracker to identify what's going on during a request (and especially, during page construction)
- [TAP5-1170](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1170) – Remove automatic injection of service id into parameters of type String
- [TAP5-1174](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1174) – Provide a ComponentEventResultProcessor that sends an error response to the client
- [TAP5-1197](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1197) – Eliminate page pooling using shared page instances that separate their structure from the mutable state
- [TAP5-1225](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1225) – Use recent Groovy/GMaven version and fix GMaven configuration in tapestry-core POM

<a id="release-notes-520--releasenotes5.2.0-newfeatures"></a>
<a id="release-notes-520--new-features"></a>

### New Features

- [TAP5-52](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-52) – Add Error component that presents validation errors of a single field
- [TAP5-56](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-56) – Tapestry should have support for IE conditional stylesheets
- [TAP5-86](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-86) – Add support for "cancel" submit buttons (which bypass client-side validation)
- [TAP5-103](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-103) – provide access to component parameters from within mixins
- [TAP5-138](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-138) – Add Zone parameter to Select component
- [TAP5-152](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-152) – Add @Translate annotation to define name of translator to be used with a bean property (rather than lookup by property type)
- [TAP5-156](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-156) – Add a @QueryParameter annotation for parameters to event handler method
- [TAP5-226](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-226) – Add annotation @SessionAttribute to map a field to a specific session attribute
- [TAP5-680](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-680) – Tapestry should provide a mixin for ensuring that the client id of a ClientElement is rendered
- [TAP5-692](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-692) – T5 should pick up validators to be applied to a field from the containing component's .properties file.
- [TAP5-801](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-801) – Add Trigger component to trigger a component event during rendering
- [TAP5-895](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-895) – Tracking issue for Tapestry/JSR-303 integration
- [TAP5-951](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-951) – Create more flexible API for testing with Selenium that allows for multiple test cases to share a single instance of Selenium/SeleniumServer/Jetty
- [TAP5-966](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-966) – TapestryFilter should be able add additional modules to the Registry to accomidate different testing (or other) execution configurations
- [TAP5-1013](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1013) – Live class reloading for service implementations
- [TAP5-1028](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1028) – Validator Macros: Combine multiple common validators into a single term
- [TAP5-1035](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1035) – In places where an invalid key is used to accessed a named value, Tapestry should report the possible names better (using HTML lists, rather than a long comma-separated string)
- [TAP5-1056](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1056) – The application global message catalog should be injectable into services
- [TAP5-1064](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1064) – Extend PropertyAccess to understand Scala style properties (which use a different naming convention than JavaBeans)
- [TAP5-1065](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1065) – Non-visual mixin to generate events as it begins/ends rendering (useful for generating matching JavaScript)
- [TAP5-1079](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1079) – Live class reloading should extend to proxied objects (such as from ObjectLocator.proxy()
- [TAP5-1084](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1084) – Zones that initially render inside a Form should support updates within the Form
- [TAP5-1095](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1095) – LocalizationSetter service should expose the supported locales as a list of Locale and as a SelectModel
- [TAP5-1097](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1097) – New annotation: @HeartbeatDeferred to mark component methods that should execute at the end of the current Heartbeat
- [TAP5-1098](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1098) – Provide new SelectModelFactory service that can automatically build a standard SelectModel from objects and property names
- [TAP5-1099](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1099) – Introduce public service responsible for handling page activation
- [TAP5-1121](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1121) – Provide an annotation to support automatic discarding of the persistent fields after a component or page method invocation
- [TAP5-1159](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1159) – Easy way to customize search locations for page and component templates
- [TAP5-1190](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1190) – New page-level events to "decorate" component event and page render links

<a id="release-notes-520--releasenotes5.2.0-taskscompleted"></a>
<a id="release-notes-520--tasks-completed"></a>

### Tasks Completed

- [TAP5-11](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-11) – CookiesImplTest does specify a domain cookie with a domain not prefixed with a . (dot)
- [TAP5-556](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-556) – Fix TranslatorSourceImplTest
- [TAP5-756](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-756) – Add ioko-tapestry-commons to the related projects list
- [TAP5-819](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-819) – remove ide-specific files from all sub-modules and add them to svn:ignore
- [TAP5-969](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-969) – Method AbstractField.createDefaultParameterBinding() should be deprecated
- [TAP5-976](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-976) – Upgrade Spring dependencies to version 3.0.0.RELEASE
- [TAP5-1081](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1081) – Remove formos references from 5.2.0 archetype
- [TAP5-1087](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1087) – Upgrade TestNG dependencies to version 5.12.1
- [TAP5-1134](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1134) – Upgrade Hibernate dependencies to 3.5.2
- [TAP5-1195](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1195) – Rename annotations @QueryParameter and @QueryParameterMapped (both introduced in 5.2.0) to more mnemonic names

<a id="release-notes-521"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/release-notes-521.html -->

<!-- page_index: 147 -->

# Release Notes 5.2.1

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.2](#release-notes-52) > [Release Notes 5.2.1](#release-notes-521)

<a id="release-notes-521--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-521--release-notes-5.2.1"></a>

# Release Notes 5.2.1

Prior releases of Tapestry attempted to "optimize" URLs, by creating relative URLs where appropriate. Unfortunately, that caused its own problems with Ajax requests, since the base URL for an Ajax request is not the same as the base URL for the enclosing page. In any case, this feature has been removed, all URLs are now complete URLs (they start with a leading slash, and include the context path and the additional path that describes the page, page activation context, etc.).

As part of this change, the *internal* RequestPathOptimizer service has been removed.

<a id="release-notes-521--releasenotes5.2.1-bugsfixed"></a>
<a id="release-notes-521--bugs-fixed"></a>

### Bugs Fixed

- [TAP5-710](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-710) – Documentation about "use of asset: and context: to reference assets directly from a template file" has errors (See TAP5-214)
- [TAP5-752](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-752) – Tapestry bin download includes lots of libraries that shouldn't be there due to licensing concerns
- [TAP5-770](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-770) – Minor documentation typos in PageLoaded annotation
- [TAP5-818](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-818) – Tapestry should properly support JDK 1.5 Generics when reading and updating properties and property expressions
- [TAP5-1004](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1004) – X-Tapestry-ErrorMessage may lead to HTTP Response Splitting
- [TAP5-1011](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1011) – When using @PageActivationContext and providing a no-args activate event handler, the handler may be called too soon
- [TAP5-1118](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1118) – Using Ajax updates to add an Upload component to an existing Form fails in IE, which still sends application/x-www-form-urlencoded encoded request
- [TAP5-1123](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1123) – Documentation in ApplicationStateManager is not quite correct: when creating an SSO it uses the full injection machinery, not necessarilly the no-args constructor
- [TAP5-1177](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1177) – In a partial page update request, if the server returns an empty string for the content, the client-side is left as is, rather than cleared
- [TAP5-1188](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1188) – Refactoring the base class of a live-reloaded service implementation can cause mysterious NoSuchMethodError exceptions
- [TAP5-1192](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1192) – IE 8 throws an exception while purging JavaScript event handlers from elements as they are destroyed
- [TAP5-1201](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1201) – PageResponseRenderer.render() should call RequestGlobabls.storeActivePageName(String pageName)
- [TAP5-1222](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1222) – Accessing a public field of a non-component object inside component code can result in a TransfomationException if the accessed field name matches the name of a component field
- [TAP5-1228](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1228) – The need to configure the tapestry.alias-mode symbol makes it awkward to start a Registry up for unit testing purposes
- [TAP5-1229](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1229) – Tapestry binary/source distributions do not include all modules
- [TAP5-1230](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1230) – SeleniumTestCase.assertFieldValue() should invoke writeErrorReport() on failure
- [TAP5-1232](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1232) – Restore injection of service id (as a String) into service decorator methods for proper backwards compatibility
- [TAP5-1235](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1235) – The interaction between a Form component and the active page can be problematic when there is an error loading a page
- [TAP5-1241](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1241) – Setting the content length of the response should automatically disable response compression
- [TAP5-1244](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1244) – Tapestry components and mixins should be converted to use JavaScriptSupport, not RenderSupport
- [TAP5-1246](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1246) – Tapestry should use SQLException.getNextException() to dig down to the "root cause"
- [TAP5-1254](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1254) – The decorateComponentEventLink event name is misspelled
- [TAP5-1258](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1258) – Maven quickstart archetype puts the wrong version in the pom.xml
- [TAP5-1260](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1260) – Document "Assets", Section "Securing Assets" is outdated
- [TAP5-1261](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1261) – Tapestry adds event handlers not only to form components, but also to simple forms
- [TAP5-1264](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1264) – Ordering of MarkupRenderer (and PartialMarkupRender) filters can cause bad interraction between ClientBehaviorSupport and Heartbeat
- [TAP5-1265](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1265) – Change the description for the quickstart archetype to just "Tapestry 5 Quickstart Project"
- [TAP5-1269](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1269) – Annotation @FactoryDefaults should be allowed on methods, for use with the @Contribute annotation
- [TAP5-1277](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1277) – Tapestry incorrectly calls PrintWriter.flush(), not close(), when writing JSON objects and arrays as the response
- [TAP5-1279](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1279) – Importing a JavaScript library that is part of a stack should import the entire stack instead of the individual library (to take advantage of JavaScript aggregation)

<a id="release-notes-521--releasenotes5.2.1-improvementsmade"></a>
<a id="release-notes-521--improvements-made"></a>

### Improvements Made

- [TAP5-363](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-363) – Cookbook: Using Tapestry to serve servlet-container error pages
- [TAP5-1233](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1233) – Live reloading of service implementation classes causes IllegalAccessError if the class contains protected or package private methods
- [TAP5-1236](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1236) – Add method Response.disableCompression() (to avoid use of a internal global key)
- [TAP5-1245](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1245) – Break out the Tapestry JSON support into an independent module
- [TAP5-1250](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1250) – Add support for creating a Flow from an Iterable (not just Collection and object array)
- [TAP5-1256](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1256) – Failed Selenium assertions should capture a screenshot (as well as the current page HTML)
- [TAP5-1266](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1266) – Bring quickstart archetype up to date in several ways

<a id="release-notes-521--releasenotes5.2.1-newfeatures"></a>
<a id="release-notes-521--new-features"></a>

### New Features

- [TAP5-910](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-910) – Cookbook: defining a new enum as a component parameter, and adding the string -> enum type coercion for it

<a id="release-notes-521--releasenotes5.2.1-taskscompleted"></a>
<a id="release-notes-521--tasks-completed"></a>

### Tasks Completed

- [TAP5-1240](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1240) – Upgrade Hibernate dependencies to 3.5.4-Final
- [TAP5-1267](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1267) – Remove the code that "optimizes" generated URLs as it is not always possible to know what the base URL is
- [TAP5-1270](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1270) – Upgrade EasyMock dependency to version 3.0

Apache Tapestry, Tapestry, Apache, the Apache feather logo, and the Apache Tapestry project logo are trademarks of The Apache Software Foundation.

<a id="release-notes-522"></a>

<!-- page_index: 148 -->

# Release Notes 5.2.2

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.2](#release-notes-52) > [Release Notes 5.2.2](#release-notes-522)

<a id="release-notes-522--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-522--release-notes-5.2.2"></a>

# Release Notes 5.2.2

It is now possible to generate an absolute URL from a Link object; an absolute URL includes the URL scheme, host, port number (if necessary) followed by the complete path. This is useful in a number of situations, such as when generating a URL that may be e-mailed to an end user.

The semantics of the `Link.toAbsoluteURL()` method has changed; in prior releases it returned a complete URL (i.e., starting with a leading slash), and the method was typically used when generating an Ajax callback URL. Starting in 5.2.2 this generates an absolute URL (as described above). In most cases, you should change code that invoked `toAbsoluteURL()` with a call to `toURL()` instead; because Tapestry no longer 'optimizes' URLs, this will work for Ajax cases as well.

Deployed Tapestry applications often run behind a firewall, the available Request information about incoming hostname and port is often not accurate (it represents the private request from the firewall server to the application server, whereas the client uses a public request to the firewall server). When using absolute URLs, you will likely want to override the BaseURLSource service to provide accurate values that reflect your application's deployment.

<a id="release-notes-522--releasenotes5.2.2-bugsfixed"></a>
<a id="release-notes-522--bugs-fixed"></a>

### Bugs Fixed

- [TAP5-708](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-708) – Element.forceAttribute uses the element's namespace to match the attribute.
- [TAP5-709](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-709) – Element.attribute(String name, String value) adds elements that already exist
- [TAP5-804](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-804) – Element#addClassName can create an additional new 'class' attribute
- [TAP5-921](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-921) – Properties defined in an Interface are not exposed by PropertyAccess for abstract classes that do not directly implement the methods
- [TAP5-1287](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1287) – Some services require a notification that they have been reloaded, so they can clean up external dependencies
- [TAP5-1293](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1293) – Whitespaces in SymbolConstants.SUPPORTED\_LOCALES cause that locales are not persised
- [TAP5-1294](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1294) – Validation macros do not work when used in @Validate annotation
- [TAP5-1297](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1297) – Client-side validation of @Pattern is broken
- [TAP5-1303](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1303) – Linking a Form to a Zone will no longer work unless the Form contains validated fields
- [TAP5-1304](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1304) – When using PropertyShadowBuilder to build a service, if the property is null, an immediate exception is needed (rather than a NullPointerException)
- [TAP5-1309](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1309) – When using a MultiZoneUpdate, Tapestry will clear the referenced zone

<a id="release-notes-522--releasenotes5.2.2-improvementsmade"></a>
<a id="release-notes-522--improvements-made"></a>

### Improvements Made

- [TAP5-538](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-538) – Add the facility to optionally disable on-focus-change triggered validation while retaining the on-form-submit validation
- [TAP5-874](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-874) – Form component should be able to render a secure URL even on an insecure page
- [TAP5-1272](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1272) – New annotations @Decorate and @Advise to identify methods that decorate or annotate services
- [TAP5-1291](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1291) – Extend Link with new methods for producing absolute URLs (that include scheme, hostname, etc.)
- [TAP5-1295](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1295) – Simplify connecting a link or form to an (enclosing) Zone
- [TAP5-1298](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1298) – BeanBlockContribution should be split into two sub-classes: EditBlockContribution and DisplayBlockContribution
- [TAP5-1300](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1300) – Define a special CSS class to prevent a client-side form from submitting normally (for Ajax use cases)
- [TAP5-1306](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1306) – Additional method for Link: addParameterValue(String,Object) that uses ContextPathEncoder to encode object value to a string
- [TAP5-1314](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1314) – SeleniumTestCase should expose the underlying CommandProcessor, to allow execution of commands not defined by the Selenium interface
- [TAP5-1315](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1315) – Allow individual SeleniumTestCases to run w/o configuring SeleniumLauncher

<a id="release-notes-522--releasenotes5.2.2-newfeaturesadded"></a>
<a id="release-notes-522--new-features-added"></a>

### New Features Added

- [TAP5-1299](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1299) – Option to disable live service reloading via a JVM system property

<a id="release-notes-522--releasenotes5.2.2-taskscompleted"></a>
<a id="release-notes-522--tasks-completed"></a>

### Tasks Completed

- [TAP5-1317](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1317) – Upgrade Hibernate dependency to latest, Hibernate 3.6.0.Final

# Release Notes 5.2.6

Tapestry 5.2.6 is a maintenance release.

<a id="release-notes-526--bugs-fixed"></a>

### Bugs Fixed

- [[TAP5-1368](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1368)] - The onFailure function provided to Tapestry.ajaxRequest does not always get invoked
- [[TAP5-1546](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1546)] - TranslatorSource incorrectly annotated with @UsesConfiguration when it should now be @UsesMappedConfiguration (as of 5.2)

- [[TAP5-1539](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1539)] - Optimize document scans used by Tapestry.FieldEventManager to not locate the label or icon until actually needed

<a id="release-notes-53"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/release-notes-53.html -->

<!-- page_index: 153 -->

# Release Notes 5.3

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3](#release-notes-53)

<a id="release-notes-53--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--release-notes-5.3"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry versions 5.2 and 5.3. To upgrade from 5.2 to 5.3, most users who are not using deprecated features will be able to just update the Maven dependency in their POM file (or [download](#release-notes-53) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please read carefully below before upgrading, and also review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions.

**Contents**

- [Breaking Changes](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-breakingchanges)
- [New Features](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-newfeatures)
- [Sub-tasks Completed](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-sub-taskscompleted)
- [Bugs Fixed](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-bugsfixed)
- [Improvements Made](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-improvementsmade)
- [New Features Implemented](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-newfeaturesimplemented)
- [Tasks Completed](#release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-taskscompleted)

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-breakingchanges"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--breaking-changes"></a>

## Breaking Changes

Tapestry now depends on Servlet API version **2.5** (prior releases worked with 2.4).

Tapestry no longer supports the ability to inject the component id into a String field. This was rarely (if ever) used functionality that caused unwanted conflicts elsewhere. When a component needs to know its component id, it may inject the [ComponentResources](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/ComponentResources.html) object, and invoke `getCompleteId()`.

Tapestry templates without a <!DOCTYPE> are treated a bit differently in Tapestry 5.3; they are treated as if they have the HTML5 doctype (`<!DOCTYPE html>`). See further notes about [component templates](#component-templates).

Many classes and interfaces that were deprecated in prior releases of Tapestry have been removed in Tapestry 5.3. See [the full list](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1432). As always, you should use your IDE to find and replace all deprecated items *before* you upgrade. The following are the most significant of these:

- @IncludeJavaScriptLibrary and @IncludeStylesheet annotations (replaced by @[Import](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/annotations/Import.html))
- @ApplicationState annotation (replaced with @[SessionState](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/annotations/SessionState.html))
- "validateForm" event triggered by Form component (replaced with "validate" event)
- Code and constants related to page pooling

In addition, some of the abstract base classes used with the tapestry-func library have changed into interfaces; you will want to recompile, but may also need to adjust your code, depending on your compiler settings and use of the standard Java @Override annotation.

Because of the upgrade to Prototype 1.7, existing JavaScript that uses `value.toJSON()` may break; replace with `Object.toJSON(value)`.

Some number of interfaces and APIs in Tapestry 5.3 will be removed in Tapestry 5.4 or later. These include:

- The [RenderSupport](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.2/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/RenderSupport.html) environmental (replaced with the [JavaScriptSupport](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/javascript/JavaScriptSupport.html) environmental)
- The [MultiZoneUpdate](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.2/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/ajax/MultiZoneUpdate.html) object, replaced with [AjaxResponseRenderer](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/ajax/AjaxResponseRenderer.html)
- ClassFactory service and ClassFab interface (replaced with the [PlasticProxyFactory](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/ioc/services/PlasticProxyFactory.html) service and [PlasticClass](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/plastic/PlasticClass.html) interface)
- The "suppress redirects" functionality, which allows component event requests to respond directly with HTML, as in Tapestry 4.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-newfeatures"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--new-features"></a>

## New Features

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-underscore.js"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--underscore.js"></a>

### Underscore.js

Tapestry now bundles [Underscore.js](http://underscorejs.org/), a useful library for expressive functional programming in the browser. It is used in *no conflict mode*, made available as the `T5._` object.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-useralerts"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--user-alerts"></a>

> [!NOTE]
> ### User Alerts

Tapestry now has a central mechanism for handling user alerts; this includes the AlertManager service and the Alerts component. Just add an Alerts component to your application's standard layout component and Tapestry takes care of the rest. Alerts can be added during both traditional and Ajax requests, and may be transient (displayed for a few seconds), normal, or sticky (persist until the user expressly dismisses them). Alerts support three severities: info, warn(ing) and error; the look and feel can be customized by overriding Tapestry's default CSS rules.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-renderingcomments"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--rendering-comments"></a>

### Rendering comments

It is now possible to have Tapestry emit rendering comments; these are comments (such as `<!--BEGIN Index:loop (context:Index.tml, line 15)-->`) that can assist you in debugging markup output on the client-side. This is enabled for all requests using the configuration symbol `tapestry.component-render-tracing-enabled`, and can be added to any request by adding the query parameter `t:component-trace=true` to the URL. This will significantly increase the size of the rendered markup, but can be very helpful with complex layouts to determine which component was responsible for which portion of the rendered page.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-adaptableservicecontributions"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--adaptable-service-contributions"></a>

### Adaptable service contributions

When making contributions to a service, you are no longer restricted to contributing a value that is assignable to the type associated with the configuration; objects of any type may be contributed, and the TypeCoercer service is used to coerce the value to the configuration's type.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-componentdebuggingimprovements"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--component-debugging-improvements"></a>

### Component debugging improvements

Because of how Tapestry instruments your pages and components, using a debugger has been difficult with Tapestry page and component classes; any mutable field shows its default value in the debugger, regardless of what has been written to the field or read out of it. In Tapestry 5.3, when in *development mode*, Tapestry now shadows values read from or written to such fields into the fields themselves (this has also been fixed in the 5.2.5 maintenance release). This shadowing does not occur in production, to avoid potential memory leaks.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-reloadingdisabledinproduction"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--reloading-disabled-in-production"></a>

### Reloading disabled in production

Tapestry no longer checks for changes to Tapestry component class files, templates, or message catalogs in production mode. It is assumed that Tapestry applications are packaged as WAR files in production, and that changing the WAR file causes the servlet container to redeploy the entire application. This change is to improve throughput and reduce memory consumption in production applications.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-string-to-enumcoercions"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--string-to-enum-coercions"></a>

### String-to-Enum coercions

The TypeCoercer service now automatically generates String-to-Enum coercions without requiring a specific contribution. This coercion is case insensitive. A contribution is still allowed, and will take priority over the default coercion, but is only necessary to support "aliases" for enum values outside those defined by the enum type itself.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-javascriptandcssminimization"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--javascript-and-css-minimization"></a>

### JavaScript and CSS minimization

A new optional library, tapestry-yuicompressor, has been added. This library adds support for compressing JavaScript libraries and CSS files using [the YUICompressor library](http://developer.yahoo.com/yui/compressor/).

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-componentclasstransformworkerservice"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--componentclasstransformworker-service"></a>

### ComponentClassTransformWorker Service

The type of this service has changed from [ComponentClassTransformWorker](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.2/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/ComponentClassTransformWorker.html) to [ComponentClassTransformWorker2](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/transform/ComponentClassTransformWorker2.html). Contributions of type ComponentClassTransformWorker will automatically be coerced to the new ComponentClassTransformWorker2 interface. However, if you use the @Contribute annotation to mark the method that makes contributions, you will need to update the annotation to indicate the new service interface. If you followed the naming convention, and named your method `contributeComponentClassTransformWorker()`, you don't need to change anything.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-ajaxresponserenderer"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--ajaxresponserenderer"></a>

### AjaxResponseRenderer

The [AjaxResponseRenderer](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/ajax/AjaxResponseRenderer.html) service makes it easier to customize the partial page response generated when updating a [Zone](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/corelib/components/Zone.html) component, replacing the [MultiZoneUpdate](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.2/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/ajax/MultiZoneUpdate.html) object as an event handler method return value.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-tapestryjavadoc"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--tapestry-javadoc"></a>

### Tapestry JavaDoc

Tapestry now includes a new library, tapestry-javadoc, that replaces the old Maven-based component report. Simply by placing a `@tapestrydoc` annotation into your component's JavaDoc, Tapestry will generate complete documentation as part of JavaDoc ... no more switching back and forth between JavaDoc and the Maven-generated component report, and no more reliance on Maven for component documentation.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-skinning-themingsupport"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--skinning-theming-support"></a>

### Skinning / Theming Support

Tapestry now adds the ability to skin and/or theme your pages and components. Its an extension of how Tapestry manages pages per locale, but adds new application-defined *axes* along with rules to find the resources. See [ComponentResourceSelector](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/pageload/ComponentResourceSelector.html) and [ComponentRequestSelectorAnalyzer](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/pageload/ComponentRequestSelectorAnalyzer.html).

There's also a new [Dynamic](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/corelib/components/Dynamic.html) component, which uses an external template, not a Tapestry template, which can be selected at runtime.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-treecomponent"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--tree-component"></a>

### Tree Component

Tapestry finally has a proper [Tree](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/corelib/components/Tree.html) component for navigating hierarchical data. It's efficient and Ajax-enabled, and fully customizable.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-formcancelledevent"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--form-cancelled-event"></a>

### Form Cancelled Event

Form components now recognize when the client-side form was cancelled. A new "cancelled" event is triggered early in the submission process, which allows the page to bypass all property updates and input validations, when desired.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-implicitorderedconfigurationconstraints"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--implicit-orderedconfiguration-constraints"></a>

### Implicit OrderedConfiguration Constraints

When using [OrderedConfiguration.add()](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/ioc/OrderedConfiguration.html) with no constraints, Tapestry will now implicitly order the added element after the previously added element, within the same method. In prior releases, such elements were added with no constraints. This makes it easier to contribute a group of related items with an implicit ordering.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-newcomponentclassvalidations"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--new-component-class-validations"></a>

### New Component Class Validations

Tapestry includes new validations of component classes to help cut down on many common errors; Tapestry now checks that component ids referenced by an event handler method (the `From`*component id* part of the method name) actually matches a component defined in the template ... this quickly identifies typos in method names. This check can be disabled with a configuration symbol, as existing 5.2 apps may have such errors and still operate (that is, they may have *dead* event handler methods that will never be invoked).

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-applicationfolder"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--application-folder"></a>

### Application Folder

Tapestry can now be configured to execute inside a folder, which can be useful when running Tapestry inside a web application that contains other servlets or filters, as a way to prevent conflicts.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-improvedquickstartarchetype"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--improved-quickstart-archetype"></a>

### Improved Quickstart Archetype

The Maven quickstart archetype has been brought up to date, and now demonstrates several new bits of common useful functionality. It also generates support for [Gradle](http://gradle.org/) as the build tool.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-whitelistedpages"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--whitelisted-pages"></a>

### Whitelisted Pages

The new @[WhitelistAccessOnly](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/annotations/WhitelistAccessOnly.html) annotation marks a page as accessible only from white-listed clients; the rules for the whitelist are [extensible](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/services/security/ClientWhitelist.html). The default rule is that only access from localhost is on the white-list. Use this annotation on pages that may expose sensitive data, such as built-in application dashboards and the like.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-pagecatalog"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--page-catalog"></a>

### Page Catalog

Tapestry applications now include a built-in "PageCatalog" page, which lists all the loaded pages of the application, with details about construction time and number of components. The page requires whitelist access (see above comment), and some functionality is only available in development mode. PageCatalog can be used to load all pages of your application, which is useful to quickly spot problems in your pages ... especially useful when upgrading from a prior release of Tapestry.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-client-sidejavascriptlogging"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--client-side-javascript-logging"></a>

### Client-Side JavaScript Logging

The integrated Blackbird client-side console has been removed. In its place are the floating console messages combined with logging to the Firebug or WebKit console. Tapestry no longer captures JavaScript initialization exceptions, so that they may be properly reported to the native console. These changes should make debugging client-side JavaScript much easier.

In addition, Tapestry will now display a pop-up window containing the full exception report for a server-side error that occurs during an Ajax request, which makes debugging server-side problems that occur as part of an Ajax request *significantly* easier. Note that this logic is tied to performing a [Zone](http://tapestry.apache.org/5.3/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/corelib/components/Zone.html) update.

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-sub-taskscompleted"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--sub-tasks-completed"></a>

## Sub-tasks Completed

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-bugsfixed"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--bugs-fixed"></a>

## Bugs Fixed

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-improvementsmade"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--improvements-made"></a>

## Improvements Made

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-newfeaturesimplemented"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--new-features-implemented"></a>

## New Features Implemented

<a id="release-notes-53--releasenotes5.3-taskscompleted"></a>
<a id="release-notes-53--tasks-completed"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.2

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3.2](#release-notes-532)

<a id="release-notes-532--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-532--release-notes-5.3.2"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.2

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry versions 5.3.1 and 5.3.2. To upgrade, just update the Maven dependency in you POM file (or [download](#download) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading. And be sure to check the [Release Notes for 5.3](#release-notes-53) and [Release Notes for 5.3.1](#release-notes-531) too.<a id="release-notes-532--releasenotes5.3.2-newfeatures"></a>
<a id="release-notes-532--new-features"></a>

## New Features

<a id="release-notes-532--releasenotes5.3.2-componentfieldvisibility"></a>
<a id="release-notes-532--component-field-visibility"></a>

### Component field visibility

In prior versions of Tapestry, all instance fields of components had to be visibility private; starting with version 5.3.2, this has been relaxed. Component fields may be protected, or package private (that is, no visibility modifier). Fields that are final, or annotated with @[Retain](http://tapestry.apache.org/current/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/annotations/Retain.html) may even be public. In any case, this makes it easier for pages to work with other pages in the same package, and for subclasses to more easily access the fields (including parameter fields, or injections) provided by base classes. This feature should be used with care, as it can lead to designs that are more difficult to maintain.

<a id="release-notes-532--releasenotes5.3.2-spring3.1upgrade"></a>
<a id="release-notes-532--spring-3.1-upgrade"></a>

### Spring 3.1 upgrade

The Spring integration module has updated the Spring dependencies to 3.1.0.RELEASE

<a id="release-notes-532--releasenotes5.3.2-textfieldcomponentsupportforhtml5values"></a>
<a id="release-notes-532--textfield-component-support-for-html5-values"></a>

### TextField component support for HTML5 values

The [TextField](http://tapestry.apache.org/current/apidocs/org/apache/tapestry5/corelib/components/TextField.html) components now support HTML5 any HTML5 type attribute.

# Release Notes 5.3.3

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3.3](#release-notes-533)

<a id="release-notes-533--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-533--release-notes-5.3.3"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.3

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry versions 5.3.2 and 5.3.3. Tapestry 5.3.3 is a drop-in replacement for prior Tapestry 5.3 releases. To upgrade, just update the Maven dependency in you POM file (or [download](#download) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading.

This is a very modest bug fix release, but it does straighten out a significant problem with Java component classes containing non-private fields.

For those developers not using Maven or Gradle, you'll be pleased to know that this release now includes the option of a binary download containing Tapestry's modules and main dependencies. More details are on the [Download](#download) page.

# Release Notes 5.3.4

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry versions 5.3.3 and 5.3.4. Tapestry 5.3.4 is a drop-in replacement for prior Tapestry 5.3 releases. To upgrade, just update the Maven dependency in you POM file (or [download](#download) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading.

This is a very modest bug fix release, including a number of minor performance improvements.

For those developers not using Maven or Gradle, you'll be pleased that we now offer the option of a binary download containing Tapestry's modules and main dependencies. More details are on the [Download](#download) page.

# Release Notes 5.3.5

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3.5](#release-notes-535)

<a id="release-notes-535--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-535--release-notes-5.3.5"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.5

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry included in version 5.3.5. Tapestry 5.3.5 is a drop-in replacement for prior Tapestry 5.3 releases. To upgrade, just update the Maven dependency in you POM file (or [download](#download) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading.

This is a very modest bug fix release, including a number of minor performance improvements. Importantly, the bundled version of [Prototype](http://prototypejs.org/) is now 1.7.1, which includes some significant improvements and bug fixes that target Internet Explorer. However, the prototype change *may* affect the functionality of the Tapestry DateField component under some versions of Internet Explorer (this issue was discovered late in the voting process).

For those developers not using Maven or Gradle, you'll be pleased that we now offer the option of a binary download containing Tapestry's modules and main dependencies. More details are on the [Download](#download) page.

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/release-notes-536.html -->

<!-- page_index: 159 -->

# Release Notes 5.3.6

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3.6](#release-notes-536)

<a id="release-notes-536--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-536--release-notes-5.3.6"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.6

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry versions 5.3.5 and 5.3.6. Tapestry 5.3.6 is a drop-in replacement for prior Tapestry 5.3 releases. To upgrade, just update the Maven dependency in your POM file (or [download](#download) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading.

This is a very modest bug fix release. Importantly, the bundled version of [Prototype](http://prototypejs.org/2012/8/8/prototype-1-7-1) has been downgraded back to version 1.7, as the new version was causing a number of issues, especially under Internet Explorer.

The main improvement is security related; Tapestry will now integrate a [hash-based message authentication code](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HMAC) (HMAC) into serialized Java object data stored on the client (generally, this means the `t:formdata` hidden field used by the Form component).

When you first run your application under 5.3.6, you will see an alert and a console error concerning the HMAC configuration. You should update your application's configuration to set a unique, private value for the [tapestry.hmac-passphrase](#configuration) configuration symbol.

And, as with any Tapestry upgrade, be sure to change your [application's version number](#configuration).

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

# Release Notes 5.3.7

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3.7](#release-notes-537)

<a id="release-notes-537--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-537--release-notes-5.3.7"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.7

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

This is the consolidated list of changes between Tapestry versions 5.3.6 and 5.3.7. Tapestry 5.3.7 is a drop-in replacement for prior Tapestry 5.3 releases. To upgrade, just update the Maven dependency in your POM file (or [download](#release-notes-537) the new JAR file) and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading.

This is a bug fix release, mainly focused on three aspects:

- make Tapestry work better under Tomcat
- minimize the possibility of clash for generated ids
- let the BeanEditor be more JSR-303 friendly

Some work has been made on the form component too.

As with any Tapestry upgrade, be sure to change your [application's version number](#configuration).

Error formatting macro: scrollbar: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

<a id="release-notes-538"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/release-notes-538.html -->

<!-- page_index: 161 -->

# Release Notes 5.3.8

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Documentation](#documentation) > [Release Notes](#release-notes) > [Release Notes 5.3.8](#release-notes-538)

<a id="release-notes-538--title"></a>
<a id="release-notes-538--release-notes-5.3.8"></a>

# Release Notes 5.3.8

**Error rendering macro 'scrollbar'**

com/opensymphony/webwork/ServletActionContext

Tapestry 5.3.8 is a drop-in replacement for prior Tapestry 5.3 releases. To upgrade, just update the dependency in your build configuration (Maven POM, Gradle build script, etc.) – or [Download](#download) the new JAR file -- and the new version will just work. However, please review the [How to Upgrade](#how-to-upgrade) instructions before upgrading.

This is a bug fix release, mainly meant to address compatibility with Java 8. The ASM library is updated to a Java 8 compatible version (5.0), however, if you wish to run this release on JRE 8, you still need to manually specify a Java 8 compatible version of javassist (such as 3.18.2-GA). With Maven you'd include:

**pom.xml (partial)**

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.javassist</groupId>
  <artifactId>javassist</artifactId>
  <version>3.18.2-GA</version>
</dependency>
```

Javassist has changed their group coordinates (from javassist to org.javassist), which further complicates the situation. You'll also have to manually exclude javassist:javassist dependency (if your dependencies are resolved automatically). With Maven, you'd add:

**pom.xml (partial)**

```xml
<exclusions>
  <exclusion>
    <artifactId>javassist</artifactId>
    <groupId>javassist</groupId>
  </exclusion>
</exclusions>
```

where needed.

As with any Tapestry upgrade, be sure to change your [application's version number](#configuration).

<a id="release-notes-538--bugs-fixed"></a>

## Bugs fixed

- [[TAP5-311](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-311)] - NPE in BeanDisplay if used in a form with a default model
- [[TAP5-983](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-983)] - CLONE -NPE in BeanDisplay if used in a form with a default model
- [[TAP5-1865](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1865)] - Chinese valication message issue
- [[TAP5-2270](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-2270)] - BeanDisplay exception when used inside a Form
- [[TAP5-2298](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-2298)] - "Any" component lacks any useful documentation
- [[TAP5-2321](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-2321)] - Tapestry 5.3.7 does not run with Java 8

<a id="release-notes-538--dependency-upgrade"></a>

## Dependency upgrade

- [[TAP5-2295](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-2295)] - Vulnerability in Tapestry-upload module due to commons-file-upload

<a id="release-notes-538--improvements-made"></a>

## Improvements made

- [[TAP5-1718](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-1718)] - Tapestry-beanvalidator isn't validating nested DTO objects / Doesn't mark the invalid fields in the UI
- [[TAP5-2157](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-2157)] - Czech translations

<a id="release-notes-538--new-features-added"></a>

## New Features added

- [[TAP5-2305](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5-2305)] - Create a @Sortable annotation for properties
