<a id="download"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/download.html -->

<!-- page_index: 204 -->

# Download

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Index](#index) > [Download](#download)

<a id="download--title"></a>
<a id="download--download"></a>

# Download

Tapestry can be downloaded in either binary or source format.

The easiest way to download Tapestry and its dependencies is using Maven, as described in [Getting Started](#getting-started).

See the [Release Notes](#release-notes) for differences between versions and how to upgrade, and [Supported Environments and Versions](#supported-environments-and-versions) for the compatibility matrix.

<a id="download--download-stablereleases"></a>
<a id="download--stable-releases"></a>

# Stable Releases

Stable releases should be relatively free of critical bugs and are considered the *safest* option if stability is a requirement.

Note that the *recommended* distribution method of binary and source JARs is via Maven repository. Binaries, source code, and Javadocs are also available as a zip archive.

<a id="download--download-tapestry5.8.x"></a>
<a id="download--tapestry-5.8.x"></a>

## Tapestry 5.8.x

Tapestry 5.8.7 was released on 9 Aug 2024. See the [release notes](#release-notes-587) for more details.

| Tapestry 5.8.7 | Mirrors | Signature | Checksum |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Binary (zip) | [Download](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-bin.zip) | [ASC](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-bin.zip.asc) | [SHA-256](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-bin.zip.sha256) |
| Source (zip) | [Download](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-sources.zip) | [ASC](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-sources.zip.asc) | [SHA-256](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-sources.zip.sha256) |
| Javadoc (zip) | [Download](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-apidocs.zip) | [ASC](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.8.7-apidocs.zip.asc) | *not available* |

**Maven Dependency**

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.apache.tapestry</groupId>
  <artifactId>tapestry-core</artifactId>
  <version>5.8.7</version>
</dependency>
```

<a id="download--download-tapestry5.7.x"></a>

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.apache.tapestry</groupId>
  <artifactId>tapestry-core</artifactId>
  <version>5.4.5</version>
</dependency>
```

<a id="download--download-tapestry5.3.x"></a>
<a id="download--tapestry-5.3.x"></a>

## Tapestry 5.3.x

Tapestry 5.3.8 was released on 20 Nov 2014. See the [release notes](#release-notes-538) for more details.

| Tapestry 5.3.8 | Mirrors | Signature | Checksum |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Binary (zip) | [Download](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-bin.zip) | [ASC](http://www.apache.org/dist/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-bin.zip.asc) | [MD5](http://www.apache.org/dist/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-bin.zip.md5) |
| Source (zip) | [Download](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-sources.zip) | [ASC](http://www.apache.org/dist/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-sources.zip.asc) | [MD5](http://www.apache.org/dist/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-sources.zip.md5) |
| Javadoc (zip) | [Download](https://downloads.apache.org/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-javadocs.zip) | [ASC](http://www.apache.org/dist/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-javadocs.zip.asc) | [MD5](http://www.apache.org/dist/tapestry/apache-tapestry-5.3.8-javadocs.zip.md5) |

**Maven Dependency**

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.apache.tapestry</groupId>
  <artifactId>tapestry-core</artifactId>
  <version>5.3.8</version>
</dependency>
```

Archives

Looking for an older version of Tapestry? Try the [archives](http://archive.apache.org/dist/tapestry/).

<a id="download--download-downloadsources"></a>
<a id="download--download-sources"></a>

# Download sources

The source download links are listed in the boxes above. In addition, the source code can be checked out anonymously from Git with the [clone](http://gitref.org/creating/#clone) command:

```java
git clone https://gitbox.apache.org/repos/asf/tapestry-5.git
```

(See [Building Tapestry from Source](#building-tapestry-from-source) for more info.)

Versions 5.3.2 and earlier are also available via Subversion, at <https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/tapestry/tapestry5/trunk/>

<a id="download--download-accessfrombehindafirewall"></a>
<a id="download--access-from-behind-a-firewall"></a>

## Access from behind a firewall

Refer to the documentation of your Git client for more information about access behind a firewall. Generally this requires configuring access through a proxy (by specifying the proxy host and port) or opening up a port on the firewall.

<a id="about"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/about.html -->

<!-- page_index: 205 -->

# About

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Index](#index) > [About](#about)

<a id="about--title"></a>
<a id="about--about"></a>

# About

Apache Tapestry is an open-source framework for creating dynamic, robust, highly scalable web applications in Java or other JVM languages. Tapestry complements and builds upon the standard Java Servlet API, and so it works in any servlet container or application server.

Tapestry is released under the [Apache Software License 2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0).

Tapestry has a long history, with the oldest code dating back to January 2000. That means a lot of releases. At this time, Tapestry releases 3 and 4 are no longer being developed; developer effort for the past several years has focused on Tapestry 5.

To learn more about Tapestry, start with the [Introduction](#introduction) or dive right in with [Getting Started](#getting-started).

<a id="about--about-thepeoplebehindtapestry"></a>
<a id="about--the-people-behind-tapestry"></a>

<a id="about--about-trainingandsupport"></a>
<a id="about--training-and-support"></a>

## Training and Support

Apache Tapestry is an open source project and as such, supported by the community. Even if you are looking for commercial support, your best bet for support is to first subscribe to Apache Tapestry user mailing list and ask there. Read more about how to [get involved](#community).<a id="about--about-commercialtrainingandsupport"></a>
<a id="about--commercial-training-and-support"></a>

### Commercial Training and Support

Training on Tapestry and Support is available directly from the source: the Tapestry project committers:

- [Thiago H. de Paula Figueiredo](http://www.arsmachina.com.br/services): Provides training and support on Tapestry and related technologies including Hibernate

<a id="about--about-communitysupport"></a>
<a id="about--community-support"></a>

### Community Support

Like all Apache projects, Tapestry uses mailing lists for most communication. You can subscribe by sending e-mail to the addresses below. For each list, there are subscribe, unsubscribe, and archive links. All Tapestry users are welcome to subscribe to any of these lists, however questions on how to *use* Tapestry in your application are best sent to the *user* mailing list.

|  | Subscribe | Unsubscribe | Apache Archive | MarkMail Archive |

| Tapestry User List | [Subscribe](mailto:users-subscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:users-unsubscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [mail-archives.apache.org](http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/tapestry-users/) | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=list%3Aorg.apache.tapestry.users) |
| Tapestry Developer List | [Subscribe](mailto:dev-subscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:dev-unsubscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [mail-archives.apache.org](http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/tapestry-dev/) | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=list%3Aorg.apache.tapestry.dev) |
| Tapestry Commits List | [Subscribe](mailto:commits-subscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:commits-unsubscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [mail-archives.apache.org](http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/tapestry-commits/) | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=list%3Aorg.apache.tapestry.commits) |
| Search Multiple Lists | - | - | - | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=#query:+page:1+state:facets) |

# Community

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Index](#index) > [Community](#community)

<a id="community--title"></a>
<a id="community--community"></a>

# Community

Tapestry has an active community of users and developers. This is an overview of how to participate, along with a list of some of the great contributions of the community members.

- [Getting Involved](#community--community-gettinginvolved)
  - [Reporting Problems / Getting Support](#community--community-reportingproblems-gettingsupport)
  - [Contributing translations for Tapestry built-in messages](#community--community-contributingtranslationsfortapestrybuilt-inmessages)
  - [Source Code Access](#community--community-sourcecodeaccess)
  - [Becoming a Contributor](#community--community-becomingacontributor)
  - [Becoming a Committer](#community--community-becomingacommitter)
- [Community Contributions](#community--community-communitycontributions)
  - [Modules](#community--community-modules)
- [Extensions](#community--community-extensions)
- [Tutorials](#community--community-tutorials)
- [IDE Integrations](#community--community-ideintegrations)

<a id="community--community-gettinginvolved"></a>
<a id="community--getting-involved"></a>

# Getting Involved

<a id="community--community-reportingproblems-gettingsupport"></a>
<a id="community--reporting-problems-getting-support"></a>

## Reporting Problems / Getting Support

Like all Apache projects, Tapestry uses mailing lists for most communication. You can subscribe by sending e-mail to the addresses below. For each list, there are subscribe, unsubscribe, and archive links. All Tapestry users are welcome to subscribe to any of these lists, however questions on how to *use* Tapestry in your application are best sent to the *user* mailing list.

|  | Subscribe | Unsubscribe | Apache Archive | MarkMail Archive |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Tapestry User List | [Subscribe](mailto:users-subscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:users-unsubscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [mail-archives.apache.org](http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/tapestry-users/) | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=list%3Aorg.apache.tapestry.users) |
| Tapestry Developer List | [Subscribe](mailto:dev-subscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:dev-unsubscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [mail-archives.apache.org](http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/tapestry-dev/) | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=list%3Aorg.apache.tapestry.dev) |
| Tapestry Commits List | [Subscribe](mailto:commits-subscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [Unsubscribe](mailto:commits-unsubscribe@tapestry.apache.org) | [mail-archives.apache.org](http://mail-archives.apache.org/mod_mbox/tapestry-commits/) | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=list%3Aorg.apache.tapestry.commits) |
| Search Multiple Lists | - | - | - | [tapestry.markmail.org](http://tapestry.markmail.org/search/?q=#query:+page:1+state:facets) |

Tapestry issues are tracked in the [Apache JIRA](https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/TAP5).

Unless your problem is clear as day, it's a good idea to discuss it on the Tapestry Users mailing list first, before adding an issue. At the same time, it's generally unlikely that a bug will be fixed *unless* a JIRA Issue is created.

Eric Raymond has a detailed [guide to asking questions the right way](http://catb.org/esr/faqs/smart-questions.html). If you are not getting a response to your problem, it's likely because you aren't asking it the right way.

Just saying something is "broken" or "failed" is not enough. How did it fail? Did it do the wrong thing? Throw an exception? Not respond in any way? What exactly did you expect to happen? All of this information should be made available when looking for help, plus context on the general problem you were trying to solve in the first place (there may be a better solution entirely). Read Eric Raymond's guide ... it's fun and informative.

<a id="community--community-contributingtranslationsfortapestrybuilt-inmessages"></a>
<a id="community--contributing-translations-for-tapestry-built-in-messages"></a>

## Contributing translations for Tapestry built-in messages

If Tapestry's built-in messages aren't available in your language, you are welcome to contribute a new translation of the message catalogs. For easy instructions, see [Localization](#localization).

<a id="community--community-sourcecodeaccess"></a>
<a id="community--source-code-access"></a>

## Source Code Access

Source code for Tapestry can be [downloaded](#download) along with pre-compiled binaries.

Tapestry uses [Git](http://git-scm.com/) to manage the project's source code.

Web access to the Tapestry repository is available as [GIT-WIP at Apache](https://git-wip-us.apache.org/repos/asf?p=tapestry-5.git).

Access using Git client:

```
$ git clone http://git-wip-us.apache.org/repos/asf/tapestry-5.git
```

(See [Building Tapestry from Source](#building-tapestry-from-source) for more info.)

<a id="community--community-becomingacontributor"></a>
<a id="community--becoming-a-contributor"></a>

## Becoming a Contributor

The best way to become a contributor is to become active on the mailing list; Tapestry is known to have an active and helpful community on the mailing list, and the more mentors we can add, the better.

If you want to help out with documentation, you must sign an [Apache Contributor License Agreement](http://www.apache.org/licenses/#clas), at which point we can grant write access to the Confluence Wiki (where official documentation is created).

Providing patches (with tests!) is another way to become a contributor. (See the [Developer Bible](#developer-bible) for important guidance on source code formatting, class naming conventions, etc.)

<a id="community--community-becomingacommitter"></a>
<a id="community--becoming-a-committer"></a>

## Becoming a Committer

Active contributors may be asked to become full committers, with write access to the source code. Generally, contributors who have been consistently active and helpful for three to six months are eligible for committer access. If you think you are in that category, don't be shy about contacting members of the Tapestry PMC (Project Management Committee).

---

<a id="community--community-communitycontributions"></a>
<a id="community--community-contributions"></a>

# Community Contributions

<a id="community--community-modules"></a>
<a id="community--modules"></a>

## Modules

[Chenille Kit](https://github.com/chenillekit/chenillekit) by Massimo Lusetti

> Collection of modules, services, utilities and components (many of which require only tapestry-ioc). Includes Accordion, ColorPicker, Editor, Kaptcha, MultipleSelect, RoundCornerContainer, ThumbNail, and many more useful components. Also provides integrations with Google services, LDAP, Lucene, Mail, Quartz, JasperReports, Bean Scripting Framework, and more.

[Equanda-tapestry5](http://equanda.org/equanda-tapestry5/) by Joachim Van der Auwera

> Components useful for building enterprise applications. Includes Accordion, Form Traversal, Tabs, TextAreaAutoExpander, TreeTable, . Among other things, these focus on easy input of data without the need for a mouse.

[ioko-tapestry-commons](http://tapestry.ioko.com/) by Ben Gidley, et al.

> Provides components for caching, cache control, and simple Flash movie integration.

[Tapx](http://tapestry.formos.com/nightly/tapx/) by Howard M. Lewis Ship

> Collection of modules and components: improved DatePicker, dynamic Tapestry templates, offline rendering using Tapestry, YUI integration, Confirm dialog mixin, Kaptcha components, and more!

[Tapestry5-jQuery](http://tapestry5-jquery.com/) by GOT5

> Tapestry5-Jquery lets you use jQuery to completely replace Prototype, Scriptaculous and the base tapestry.js script.

[Exanpe-T5-Lib](http://exanpe-t5-lib.appspot.com/) by Laurent Guérin et Julien Maupoux

> A library of components: accordion, ajax loader, slider, tab view, secured password, color picker, tooltip, hideable panel and more! Live demo is provided.

[FlowLogix](http://code.google.com/p/flowlogix/) by Lenny Primak

> a collection of components, services and utilities that integrates Tapestry into JEE environments and provides other commonly needed functionality.

[More Modules](#modules)...

<a id="community--community-extensions"></a>
<a id="community--extensions"></a>

# Extensions

[Ars Machina](http://www.arsmachina.com.br/) by Thiago H. de Paula Figueiredo

> Tapestry/Hibernate extensions for Generic DAOs, standard CRUD interfaces, and user access logging and tracking.

[Tynamo project](http://tynamo.org/) by Tynamo Team / Kalle Korhonen & Alejandro Scandroli

> Tynamo is model-driven, full-stack web framework based on Tapestry 5, allowing you to jump directly from your Hibernate entities to a full-blown CRUD application. Tynamo provides several modules, including tapestry-model, tapestry-conversations, tapestry-hibernate-seedentity, tapestry-resteasy and tapestry-security.

<a id="community--community-tutorials"></a>
<a id="community--tutorials"></a>

# Tutorials

[Tapestry JumpStart](https://tapestry-jumpstart.org/jumpstart) by Geoff Callender

> JumpStart is an easy way to learn Tapestry by example. It's an online demo application, and also a downloadable open source app ready for you to explore and modify.

[Tapestry Stitch](http://t5stitch-lazan.rhcloud.com/) by Lance

> Sample components and concepts in Apache Tapestry 5

[Shams Examples](http://code.google.com/p/shams/) by Mohammad H. Shamsi

> A variety of examples of Tapestry 5 pages and components.

[Community's Wiki](http://wiki.apache.org/tapestry/FrontPage) (Moin Moin)

> The wiki contains a wealth of user-generated tips and techniques for using Tapestry

<a id="community--community-ideintegrations"></a>
<a id="community--ide-integrations"></a>

# IDE Integrations

[Eclipse-tapestry5-plugin](https://github.com/anjlab/eclipse-tapestry5-plugin)

> An Eclipse plugin for Tapestry 5 by Dmitry Gusev, with a "Quick Switch" between templates and component classes, a Tapestry Context view, and many other convenience features. This is currently the best choice for Eclipse users.

[Tapestry Tools](http://code.google.com/p/tapestrytools/wiki/Install_Guide_Lightweight_TapestryTools) by Gavin Lei

> Tapestry Tools is an Eclipse plugin for Tapestry 5 which has been built by Gavin Lei within the timeframe of GSoC 2012.

[IntelliJ 10](http://www.jetbrains.com/idea) by JetBrains

> IntelliJ has Tapestry 5 support included [right out of the box](http://www.jetbrains.com/idea/features/tapestry_5.html).

[loom-t5](http://code.google.com/p/loom-t5/) by Chris Scheid

> Eclipse plugin for building Tapestry 5 projects

[Code Completion in Eclipse](http://wiki.apache.org/tapestry/Tapestry5JSPEditorEclipse)

> How to use the built in JSP Eclipse Editor and a custom tld file to get Tapestry 5 code completion in Eclipse

<a id="books"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/books.html -->

<!-- page_index: 207 -->

# Books

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Index](#index) > [Books](#books)

<a id="books--title"></a>
<a id="books--books"></a>

# Books

**We think you will love Tapestry!** Give us 20 minutes and **[follow our tutorial](#tapestry-tutorial)**.

<a id="books--books-booksontapestry"></a>
<a id="books--books-on-tapestry"></a>

## Books on Tapestry

<a id="books--books-plugintapestry"></a>
<a id="books--plugin-tapestry"></a>

### PlugIn Tapestry

![](assets/images/portada-pugintapestry-thumb_18c8c6c76b056f2e.png)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 5 (Version 5.4)
- **Published:** 2014
- **Author:** Picodotdev
- **Language:** Spanish
- **Pages:** 289
- **More information:** <http://picodotdev.github.io/blog-bitix/2014/02/libro-sobre-desarrollo-de-aplicaciones-con-apache-tapestry/>

As the first book to cover Tapestry 5.4 features, and the only up-to-date Tapestry book in Spanish, this book features material on the new JavaScript abstraction layer and the use of RequireJS and JavaScript modules, in addition to in-depth coverage of many other Tapestry topics. Available in several formats (PDF, epub, mobi HTML)

---

<a id="books--books-tapestry5-rapidwebapplicationdevelopmentinjava"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-5-rapid-web-application-development-in-java"></a>

### Tapestry 5 - Rapid web application development in Java

[![](assets/images/tapestry5-rapid_ef302e643466d6bb.jpg)](http://www.tapestry5book.com/)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 5 (Version 5.3)
- **Published:** 2012
- **Author:** Igor Drobiazko
- **Language:** English
- **Pages:** 482
- **More information:** [http://www.tapestry5book.com/](https://web.archive.org/web/20180413075519/http://www.tapestry5book.com/)

Tapestry 5 - Rapid web application development in Java is a comprehensive guide, introducing Apache Tapestry and its innovative approach to building modern web applications. The book walks you through Tapestry 5, from a simple Hello World application to rich Ajax-enabled applications. Written by a core committer, this book provides deep insight into the architecture of Tapestry 5. It not only shows you *how* to achieve specific goals but also teaches you the "why". You learn how to build modern, scalable Web 2.0 application with a component-oriented approach. This book also shows how Tapestry brings scripting language productivity within reach of Java developers without sacrificing any of Java's inherent speed and power.

Table of Contents

1. Introducing Tapestry
2. Tapestry templates
3. Page navigation
4. Developing stateful applications
5. Localization
6. Creating Forms with Tapestry
7. Converting and validating user input
8. Generating UI for JavaBeans
9. Developing reusable components
10. Mixins
11. Creating Ajax-enabled applications
12. Persisting domain objects
13. Integrating Spring framework
14. Testing Tapestry applications
15. Tapestry IoC and Dependency Injection

---

<a id="books--books-tapestry5:dieentwicklungvonwebanwendungenmitleichtigkeit"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-5:-die-entwicklung-von-webanwendungen-mit-leichtigkeit"></a>

### Tapestry 5: Die Entwicklung von Webanwendungen mit Leichtigkeit

![](assets/images/tapestry5-drobiazko_4efafa0d8079be9b.jpg)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 5 (Version 5.1.0.5)
- **Published:** 2009
- **Author:** Igor Drobiazko
- **Language:** German

This is the only Tapestry 5 book written in German. It is a definitive introduction to Tapestry 5 written by Igor Drobiazko, a committer of Tapestry. The book covers:

- Getting Started with Tapestry 5
- Concepts of the framework
- Localization/internationalization
- Creating Forms
- Generation of user interfaces for JavaBeans
- Writing own components and mixins
- Ajax
- Writing tests for Tapestry applications
- Hibernate and Spring integration
- Dependency Injection and Tapestry IoC
- AOP and bytecode manipuation

---

<a id="books--books-tapestry5:buildingwebapplications"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-5:-building-web-applications"></a>

### Tapestry 5: Building Web Applications

![](assets/images/packtpub-cover_a6b33dab126cfa99.png)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 5
- **Published:** 2008
- **Author:** Alexander Kolesnikov
- **Language:** English

Alexander follows up his long series of Tapestry 4 tutorials with the *first* book on Tapestry 5.

---

<a id="books--books-tapestrycompletereference"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-complete-reference"></a>

### Tapestry Complete Reference

![](assets/images/t4chinese_9b300a78ce9638a5.jpg)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 4
- **Published:** 2007
- **Author:** A. Dong
- **Language:** Chinese

The first Chinese-language book to cover Tapestry (though Tapestry in Action has been translated). as a non-Chinese speaker, it's a bit hard to know what's between the covers, but looks like it gets into the nitty-gritty of Tapestry, Spring and Hibernate.

---

<a id="books--books-tapestry101"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-101"></a>

### Tapestry 101

![](assets/images/tapestry101_9fa65b58dad0c0a2.jpg)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 4 (Spring/Hibernate/Hivemind/Tacos(ajax)
- **Publisher:** SourceBeat
- **Published:** 2006
- **Authors:** Warner Onstine

Tapestry is an open-source web application framework written in Java. This framework is different from "standard" MVC frameworks *(like Struts or WebWork)* in that it is more of a component-oriented framework. A component-oriented framework *(like JavaServer Faces)* allows you to create re-usable components that you can then re-use on other projects with the ability to re-configure these components to suit a specific purpose.

Tapestry 101 will introduce readers to this framework that is growing in popularity more and more. The focus of this book is to guide you through designing and implementing your application and providing information that you can improve on it with your own custom components.

Howard Lewis Ship *(creator of Tapestry)* provides the Foreword to the book and adds:

*"Tapestry 101's focus is about getting things done, from a user's perspective not a Tapestry developer's. It covers the issues you'll face building real applications."*

---

<a id="books--books-beginningpojos:lightweightjavawebdevelopmentusingplainoldjavaobjectsinspring-hibernate-andtapestry"></a>
<a id="books--beginning-pojos:-lightweight-java-web-development-using-plain-old-java-objects-in-spring-hibernate-and-tapestry"></a>

### Beginning POJOs: Lightweight Java Web Development Using Plain Old Java Objects in Spring, Hibernate, and Tapestry

![](assets/images/beginning-pojos_1e9e84bb5e5f7cfc.gif)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 4
- **Publisher:** Apress
- **Published:** 2006
- **Authors:** Brian Sam-Bodden

This book guides you through the construction of complex but lightweight enterprise Java-based web applications. Such applications are centered around several major open source lightweight frameworks, including Spring, Hibernate, Tapestry, and JBoss. The Tapestry chapter (ch.7, p.239-304) covers:

- Installation and Configuration
- Pages and Components
- Forms and Form Components
- Hivemind Services and Application State Objects
- Annotations
- Ajax-enabling applications with Tacos

---

<a id="books--books-enjoyingwebdevelopmentwithtapestry"></a>
<a id="books--enjoying-web-development-with-tapestry"></a>

### Enjoying Web Development with Tapestry

![](assets/images/ewdt-tong-cover_9a0b604a9d7d4b66.jpg)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 4.1/4/3
- **Publisher:** Agile Skills
- **Published:** 2006
- **Authors:** [Kent Tong](http://www.agileskills2.org/EWDT/)

Enjoying Web Development with Tapestry by Kent Tong covers Tapestry 4.1 (with AJAX). Previous editions covered 4.0 and 3.0. Available in PDF and hard-copy formats.

- Getting Started with Tapestry
- Using Forms
- Validating Input
- Creating an e-Shop
- Creating Custom Components
- Supporting Other Languages
- Using the Table Component
- Handling File Downloads and Uploads
- Providing a Common Layout
- Using Javascript
- Building Interactive Forms with AJAX
- Test Driven Development with HtmlUnit
- Database and Concurrency Issues
- Using Hibernate
- Integrating with Struts

---

<a id="books--books-tapestrywebanwendungenmitdemapacheframework"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-webanwendungen-mit-dem-apache-framework"></a>

### Tapestry Webanwendungen mit dem Apache Framework

![](assets/images/tapestry-german-cover_584ffd938b0cc10d.gif)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 3
- **Publisher:** S&S
- **Published:** 2004
- **Authors:** Stefan Edlich,Patrick Kunert

Tapestry Webanwendungen mit dem Apache Framework is a fast-paced guide to using Tapestry 3, focusing on combining Tapestry with other open-source frameworks, as well as developing Tapestry applications using [Spindle](http://spindle.sourceforge.net/). Covers:

- Web Frameworks, JavaServer Faces, .NET Web Forms und Tapestry
- Installation und Einbindung in Tomcat und JBoss
- Einstieg in Tapestry, HelloWorld
- Aufbau und Anwendung von Komponenten
- Formular-Validierung, JavaScript-Support, Internationalisierung
- Tapestry-Scope-Objekte (Visit, Global), Engines
- Inspector, Eclipse Plug-in Spindle
- Persistenz für Tapestry: JDO, Hibernate, Toplink und exotische Mapper

---

<a id="books--books-tapestryinaction"></a>
<a id="books--tapestry-in-action"></a>

### Tapestry in Action

![](assets/images/tapestry-in-action_69450cd50b1fc3b0.png)

- **Covers:** Tapestry 3
- **Publisher:** Manning Publications
- **Published:** 2004
- **Authors:** [Howard M. Lewis Ship](http://howardlewisship.com/)

Tapestry in Action is the definitive introduction to Tapestry 3 written by Howard Lewis Ship, the creator of Tapestry. It covers:

- Tapestry's Component Object Model
- How to write new components
- How to configure third party components
- Dynamic JavaScript integration
- Form validation
- Tapestry/JSP integration
- Localization/internationalization
- J2EE integration

---

<a id="key-features"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://tapestry.apache.org/key-features.html -->

<!-- page_index: 209 -->

# Key Features

[Apache Tapestry](#index) > [Index](#index) > [Key Features](#key-features)

<a id="key-features--title"></a>
<a id="key-features--key-features"></a>

# Key Features

<a id="key-features--keyfeatures-javapower"></a>
<a id="key-features--java-power"></a>

### Java Power

Tapestry pages and components are simple Java POJOs, with easy access to all Java language features and the vast Java ecosystem. Thanks to Java's advanced concurrency API, Tapestry handles requests *fast* without sacrificing security or stability.

<a id="key-features--keyfeatures-scriptingease"></a>
<a id="key-features--scripting-ease"></a>

### Scripting Ease

Tapestry features *live class reloading*: change your Java code, refresh the browser and see the changes... instantly! Have your cake and eat it too: the speed and depth of Java, the agile development style of Ruby or Python.

<a id="key-features--keyfeatures-superproductivity"></a>
<a id="key-features--super-productivity"></a>

### Super Productivity

Simple POJO classes, streamlined templates, live class reloading, state-of-the-art exception reporting, first-class Ajax support, and a big library of built-in components: Tapestry is designed from the ground up to give you great productivity.

---
